sections.title: Use
rows: To express a wish about the present or a general situation
Maria wishes she **lived** near the sea.
I wish I **wasn't** so nervous before exams.
To express regret about something that happened in the past
He wishes he **had listened** to his teacher's advice.
I wish we **hadn't stayed** out so late last night.
To express annoyance about someone's behaviour, or to complain
I wish the neighbours **would stop** making so much noise.
I wish you **wouldn't leave** your things everywhere.
To express wishes about what is possible now or in the future
She wishes she **could play** the piano.
I wish I **could stay** longer, but I have to leave.

sections.title: Be careful
items: We don't use would when wishing for something about ourselves: I wish I knew the answer. (not I wish I would know the answer.)
For situations that might really happen in the future, use hope instead of wish: I hope the weather is nice tomorrow.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: A: Complete with the correct form
exercises.title: A: Complete with the correct form
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence using wish and the verb in brackets.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence using wish and the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... (know) how to cook better.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present wish, so the correct answer is "knew".

wrongOptions.know: The bare infinitive (know) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conjugate (know) to match the subject and the time clues in the sentence.

items.sentence: Ella wishes she ...... (not be) afraid of spiders.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present wish, so the correct answer is "wasn't".

wrongOptions.not be: The bare infinitive (not be) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conjugate (not be) to match the subject and the time clues in the sentence.

items.sentence: We wish we ...... (can) travel around the world.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ability wish, so the correct answer is "could".

wrongOptions.can: The bare infinitive (can) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modals and semi-modals express ability, obligation, permission, or possibility — match the nuance in the sentence.

items.sentence: He wishes he ...... (take) more photos on holiday.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past regret, so the correct answer is "had taken".

wrongOptions.taken: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.take: The bare infinitive (take) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Comparatives compare two things (-er or more + adjective).

items.sentence: I wish my brother ...... (not leave) his clothes on the floor.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is annoying habit, so the correct answer is "wouldn't leave".

wrongOptions.not leave: The bare infinitive (not leave) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conjugate (not leave) to match the subject and the time clues in the sentence.

items.sentence: Sophie wishes she ...... (not lose) her keys yesterday.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past regret, so the correct answer is "hadn't lost".

wrongOptions.not lose: The bare infinitive (not lose) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Present perfect does not combine with finished past-time words like yesterday or last week.
explanationContent.usefulTip: A finished past-time expression usually requires the past simple, not a perfect tense.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past simple is used for finished actions at a specific past time — watch irregular past forms.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... (be) more confident.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present state, so the correct answer is "was".

wrongOptions.be: The bare infinitive (be) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Comparatives compare two things (-er or more + adjective).

items.sentence: Daniel wishes he ...... (can play) the guitar.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ability, so the correct answer is "could play".

wrongOptions.can play: The bare infinitive (can play) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modals and semi-modals express ability, obligation, permission, or possibility — match the nuance in the sentence.

exercises.title: B: Choose the correct option
exercises.instructions: Choose the best option for each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the best option for each sentence.

items.originalSentence: I wish I **understand / understood / had understood** maths better.
items.completedSentence: I wish I understood maths better.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "understood" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "understood" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "understand" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.understand: "understand" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("maths better.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.understand: "understand" doesn't fit here → "understood" does, because it does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("maths better.").
optionContrast.had understood: "had understood" doesn't fit here → "understood" does, because Only "understood" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

items.originalSentence: She wishes she **wouldn't be / weren't / hadn't been** so busy every weekend.
items.completedSentence: She wishes she weren't so busy every weekend.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "weren't" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "weren't" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "wouldn't be" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.wouldn't be: "wouldn't be" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("so busy every weekend.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.wouldn't be: "wouldn't be" doesn't fit here → "weren't" does, because "wouldn't be" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("so busy every weekend.").
optionContrast.hadn't been: "hadn't been" doesn't fit here → "weren't" does, because Only "weren't" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

items.originalSentence: I wish I **can / could / had been able** ski.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "could" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "could" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "can" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.can: "can" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("ski.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.can: "can" doesn't fit here → "could" does, because it does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("ski.").
optionContrast.had been able: "had been able" doesn't fit here → "could" does, because Only "could" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

items.originalSentence: I wish you **don't leave / would stop / had stopped** making that noise.
items.completedSentence: I wish you would stop making that noise.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "would stop" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "would stop" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "don't leave" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.don't leave: "don't leave" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("making that noise.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.don't leave: "don't leave" doesn't fit here → "would stop" does, because "don't leave" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("making that noise.").
optionContrast.had stopped: "had stopped" doesn't fit here → "would stop" does, because Only "would stop" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

items.originalSentence: They wish they **visited / had visited / would visit** the museum yesterday.
items.completedSentence: They wish they had visited the museum yesterday.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "had visited" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "had visited" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "visited" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.visited: "visited" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("the museum yesterday.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.visited: "visited" doesn't fit here → "had visited" does, because it does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("the museum yesterday.").
optionContrast.would visit: "would visit" doesn't fit here → "had visited" does, because Only "had visited" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

items.originalSentence: If only I **am / were / had been** more organised.
items.completedSentence: If only I were more organised.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "were" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "were" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "am" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.am: "am" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("more organised.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

optionContrast.am: "am" doesn't fit here → "were" does, because it does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("more organised.").
optionContrast.had been: "had been" doesn't fit here → "were" does, because Only "were" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: C: Rewrite with wish or if only
exercises.title: C: Rewrite with wish or if only
exercises.instructions: Rewrite each sentence so it keeps the same meaning.
exercises.studentInstruction: Rewrite each sentence so it keeps the same meaning.

items.sentence: I don't know how to dance. → ......
items.answer: I wish I knew how to dance.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I knew how to dance.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present wish, so the correct answer is "I wish I knew how to dance.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "I wish I knew how to dance.", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: My sister always borrows my clothes. → ......
items.answer: I wish my sister wouldn't borrow my clothes.
acceptedAnswers: I wish my sister wouldn't borrow my clothes.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is annoying habit, so the correct answer is "I wish my sister wouldn't borrow my clothes.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "I wish my sister wouldn't borrow my clothes.", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: I forgot my homework yesterday. → ......
items.answer: I wish I had remembered my homework yesterday.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had remembered my homework yesterday.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past regret, so the correct answer is "I wish I had remembered my homework yesterday.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Present perfect does not combine with finished past-time words like yesterday or last week.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past simple is used for finished actions at a specific past time.

items.sentence: I can't drive a car. → ......
items.answer: I wish I could drive a car.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I could drive a car.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ability, so the correct answer is "I wish I could drive a car.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "I wish I could drive a car.", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modal verbs express different degrees of possibility, ability, and permission.

items.sentence: It may be sunny tomorrow (possible). → ......

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is possible future, so the correct answer is "I hope it's sunny tomorrow.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "I hope it's sunny tomorrow.", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Read the verb in brackets and choose the tense/form that matches the time clues in the sentence.

items.sentence: I'm not famous. → ......

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present state, so the correct answer is "If only I was famous.".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "If only I was famous.", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: D: Error correction
exercises.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.displayPrompt: I wish I would know the answer.

cues: I wish I would know the answer.

items.answer: I wish I knew the answer.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I knew the answer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: After wish, so the sentence is "I wish I knew the answer.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: I wish I would know the answer. → I wish I knew the answer.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.After: Check how "After" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.wish: Check how "wish" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: In reported speech, change time and place words (tomorrow → the next day, here → there).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

items.displayPrompt: He wishes he would be stronger.

cues: He wishes he would be stronger.

items.answer: He wishes he was stronger./He wishes he were stronger.
acceptedAnswers: He wishes he was stronger./He wishes he were stronger.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Present state, so the sentence is "He wishes he was stronger./He wishes he were stronger.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: He wishes he would be stronger. → He wishes he was stronger./He wishes he were stronger.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.Present: Check how "Present" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.state: Check how "state" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

items.displayPrompt: I wish you will be quieter.

cues: I wish you will be quieter.

items.answer: I wish you would be quieter.
acceptedAnswers: I wish you would be quieter.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Annoying habit, so the sentence is "I wish you would be quieter.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: I wish you will be quieter. → I wish you would be quieter.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.Annoying: Check how "Annoying" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.habit: Check how "habit" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

items.displayPrompt: She wishes she went to bed earlier last night.

cues: She wishes she went to bed earlier last night.

items.answer: She wishes she had gone to bed earlier last night.
acceptedAnswers: She wishes she had gone to bed earlier last night.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Past, so the sentence is "She wishes she had gone to bed earlier last night.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: She wishes she went to bed earlier last night. → She wishes she had gone to bed earlier last night.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice — be + past participle when the action matters more than who did it.

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: In reported speech, change time and place words (tomorrow → the next day, here → there).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

items.displayPrompt: I wish I could won the competition.

cues: I wish I could won the competition.

items.answer: I wish I had won the competition.
acceptedAnswers: I wish I had won the competition.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Past result, so the sentence is "I wish I had won the competition.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: I wish I could won the competition. → I wish I had won the competition.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Reported speech backshifts tenses and time/place words (now → then, tomorrow → the next day, here → there).

wrongOptions.Past: Check how "Past" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.result: Check how "result" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Match the tense shift shown in the explanation — one verb often moves back one step in time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

items.displayPrompt: I wish the test is easy tomorrow.

cues: I wish the test is easy tomorrow.

items.answer: I hope the test is easy tomorrow.
acceptedAnswers: I hope the test is easy tomorrow.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Possible future, so the sentence is "I hope the test is easy tomorrow.".
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Prompt: I wish the test is easy tomorrow. → I hope the test is easy tomorrow.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Build a grammatically complete sentence that matches the prompt: I hope the test is easy tomorrow.

wrongOptions.Possible: Check how "Possible" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.
wrongOptions.future: Check how "future" changes in the target sentence — tense or word form may shift.

explanationContent.commonMistake: In reported speech, change time and place words (tomorrow → the next day, here → there).
explanationContent.usefulTip: Write one complete sentence with correct punctuation — capital letter at the start and a full stop at the end.

exercises.title: E: Key word transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word given.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word given.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... enough money to buy a laptop. **HAD**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present wish, so the correct answer is "had".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: He wishes he ...... more before the match. **PRACTISED**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past regret, so the correct answer is "had practised".

wrongOptions.practised: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost — use past perfect for the first of two past actions.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "had practised", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Comparatives and superlatives compare people, things, or actions.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... at remembering names. **WERE**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is present state, so the correct answer is "were better".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "were better", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: I wish the dog ...... all night. **BARK**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is annoying habit, so the correct answer is "wouldn't bark".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "wouldn't bark", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: If only I ...... play chess. **COULD**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ability, so the correct answer is "could".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modal verbs express different degrees of possibility, ability, and permission.

items.sentence: We wish we ...... our work earlier. **SAVED**

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past regret, so the correct answer is "had saved".

wrongOptions.saved: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost — use past perfect for the first of two past actions.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use past perfect when one past action happened before another.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Wish + past simple/past perfect expresses regret about the present or past.
